questions.subject: Physics
questions.q: Identify the correct statements from the following.
options: Diamagnetism arises from the partial alignment of permanent magnetic moments.
The magnetic field generated by an induced magnetic moment is directed opposite to the applied field.
In free space, the magnetizing field intensity $H$ remains always zero.
Diamagnetism is present in all materials.
questions.solution:
Diamagnetism arises due to the induced change in the orbital motion of electrons when an external magnetic field is applied. It does not arise from permanent magnetic moments. Thus, statement (1) is incorrect.

According to Lenz's law, the induced magnetic moment in a diamagnetic material develops in a direction such that it opposes the applied magnetic field. Thus, statement (2) is correct.

In free space, the magnetic induction $B$ and magnetizing field intensity $H$ are related by $B = \mu_0 H$. If a magnetic field exists in free space, $H$ is non-zero. Thus, statement (3) is incorrect.

Since all materials are made of atoms containing orbiting electrons, diamagnetism is a universal property present in all materials. In paramagnetic and ferromagnetic materials, the weak diamagnetic effect is simply masked by stronger magnetic effects. Thus, statement (4) is correct.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.q: Identify the pair(s) of physical quantities that possess identical dimensions.
options: Magnetizing field intensity $H$ and intensity of magnetization $I$
Magnetic field $B$ and intensity of magnetization $I$
Magnetic field $B$ and magnetizing field intensity $H$
questions.solution:
Magnetizing field intensity $H$ and intensity of magnetization $I$ both have dimensions $[L^{-1} A]$.

Longitudinal strain is the ratio of change in length to original length, making it a dimensionless quantity $[M^{0} L^{0} T^{0}]$.

Magnetic susceptibility $\chi$ is defined as the ratio of intensity of magnetization to magnetizing field intensity ($\chi = \dfrac{I}{H}$). Since both $I$ and $H$ have the same dimensions, $\chi$ is also dimensionless $[M^{0} L^{0} T^{0}]$.

Magnetic field $B$ has dimensions $[M T^{-2} A^{-1}]$, which differs from $I$ and $H$.

Therefore, the pairs of physical quantities that possess identical dimensions are magnetizing field intensity and intensity of magnetization, as well as longitudinal strain and magnetic susceptibility.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.q: The atoms of a certain material have a permanent magnetic moment equal to zero. The material
questions.solution:
If the atoms of a material have a permanent magnetic moment equal to zero, it implies that the net magnetic moment of each individual atom is zero.

Paramagnetic and ferromagnetic materials require their constituent atoms to possess a permanent, non-zero magnetic moment.

In the absence of a permanent magnetic moment, the material can only exhibit diamagnetism. When an external magnetic field is applied, an induced magnetic moment is set up in a direction opposite to the applied field due to the change in orbital motion of electrons.

Therefore, the material must be diamagnetic.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.q: Select the correct statements from the following.
options: All atoms possess a magnetic moment.
All protons possess a magnetic moment.
All nuclei possess a magnetic moment.
All electrons possess a magnetic moment.
questions.solution:
Statement (1) is incorrect. Atoms with paired electrons and zero nuclear spin (e.g., $^{4}_{2}\text{He}$, $^{12}_{6}\text{C}$) have a net magnetic moment of zero.

Statement (2) is correct. A proton is a spin-$1/2$ particle and possesses an intrinsic magnetic moment.

Statement (3) is incorrect. Nuclei with an even number of protons and an even number of neutrons (even-even nuclei) have zero nuclear spin and zero magnetic moment.

Statement (4) is correct. An electron is a spin-$1/2$ particle and possesses an intrinsic magnetic moment.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.q: If the atoms of a material possess a non-zero permanent magnetic moment, then the material
questions.solution:
Atoms of diamagnetic materials have zero permanent magnetic moment.

Atoms of paramagnetic and ferromagnetic materials possess a non-zero permanent magnetic moment.

Therefore, if the atoms of a material possess a non-zero permanent magnetic moment, the material can be either paramagnetic or ferromagnetic.

Thus, the material may be paramagnetic.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.q: As a ferromagnetic material undergoes a hysteresis loop, its magnetic susceptibility
questions.solution:
The magnetic susceptibility $\chi$ is defined as the ratio of magnetization $M$ to the magnetizing field intensity $H$, given by $\chi = \dfrac{M}{H}$.

At the point of retentivity on the hysteresis loop, $H = 0$ while $M \neq 0$. This gives $\chi = \dfrac{M}{0} \rightarrow \infty$. Thus, the susceptibility can be infinite.

At the point of coercivity, $M = 0$ while $H \neq 0$. This gives $\chi = \dfrac{0}{H} = 0$. Thus, the susceptibility can be zero.

In the regions of the loop where $M$ and $H$ have opposite signs (such as the second and fourth quadrants), the ratio $\dfrac{M}{H}$ is negative, meaning $\chi < 0$. Thus, the susceptibility can be negative.

Since $\chi$ varies continuously throughout the hysteresis loop, it does not maintain a fixed value.
